name: UI/UX Digital Design Essentials

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The parsed course list is incoherent with the stated credential: for a UI/UX essentials certificate the 174 referenced courses and every sampled course are bioinformatics (BNFO 601-726) and computer-science systems/security/theory courses (CS 608 Cryptography, CS 630 Operating System Design, CS 643 Cloud Computing, CS 647 Counter Hacking, CS 650 Computer Architecture). No UI, UX, interaction-design, or visual-design course appears at all, indicating the catalog page aggregated an unrelated program-wide course dump rather than the certificate's actual curriculum.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the entry with the genuine UI/UX course list (the DD 640 / IS 661 / DD 634 UX courses that exist elsewhere in this catalog) and remove the unrelated bioinformatics and CS systems courses.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 3, but missingCourseCount is 15 with dangling references to undergraduate CS and program courses (CS 114, CS 241, CS 280, CS 332, CS 350, CS 435, MATH 333, YWCC 590-592, several 790-level research courses); more fundamentally the prerequisite graph that exists belongs to a CS curriculum, not a UI/UX certificate, so its structure is not meaningful for this credential.
dimensions.suggestion: Once the correct UI/UX courses are loaded, rebuild the prerequisite graph from those courses and resolve the 15 dangling references.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes are present, and because the catalog data does not surface the actual UI/UX curriculum, a reader cannot even infer the intended competencies from the courses shown.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 UI/UX learning outcomes (user research, wireframing/prototyping, usability testing, accessible visual design) alongside the corrected course list.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 3.0, which is implausible for a certificate (essentials certificates are typically ~12 credits / 4 courses), while 174 courses are referenced and summedTermCredits is null; the stated total, the reference count, and the credential type are mutually inconsistent, so credit transparency is effectively broken.
dimensions.suggestion: Correct the total to the real requirement (e.g., 12 credits / 4 courses) and scope the referenced courses to only those in the certificate.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The catalog page cannot orient a prospective student: it lists 174 unrelated graduate courses spanning bioinformatics, cryptography, cloud computing, and computer architecture instead of any UI/UX content, states only 3 total credits, and includes empty descriptions (e.g., BNFO 698 'ST:' with a blank excerpt). This is a broken entry rather than a thin one.
dimensions.suggestion: Replace the aggregated course dump with a curated UI/UX plan of study, correct credit total, and a short credential overview.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Relevance to the stated UI/UX field cannot be demonstrated because no UI/UX course is surfaced; the courses that do appear (CS 634 Data Mining, CS 643 Cloud Computing, CS 645 Security) are individually current CS topics but irrelevant to a digital-design certificate, so the entry fails to evidence currency in its own discipline.
dimensions.suggestion: Populate the certificate with current UI/UX practice courses (design systems, Figma prototyping, accessibility) so its industry relevance can actually be assessed.

descriptionQuality.justification: Among the sampled (wrong) courses many CS descriptions are actually substantive, but several are empty or placeholder (BNFO 698 'ST:' has a blank excerpt; BNFO 700B/701 are boilerplate project/thesis stubs), and critically none describes UI/UX content, so description quality for the stated certificate cannot be established.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisite fields are unreliable: 15 dangling references exist, and some prerequisiteRaw values are malformed description fragments rather than prerequisites (e.g., CS 602 lists 'advanced Web-based programming with an emphasis on the Java language and platform' as its prerequisite), so prerequisites are neither unambiguous nor internally consistent.

missingTopics.rationale: A UI/UX essentials certificate must cover interaction principles and usability heuristics, yet no such course is surfaced in the parsed data.

missingTopics.topic: User research and usability testing methods
missingTopics.rationale: Core UX practice (interviews, personas, moderated/unmoderated usability testing) is absent from what the catalog shows, though UX courses like IS 661 exist elsewhere in this catalog.
Hands-on prototyping with industry tools is essential to a UI/UX credential and is not represented among the listed courses.
Accessible design is now a baseline professional and legal expectation for UI/UX work and should be an explicit topic.
UI fundamentals (grids, type, color, component/design systems) underpin the 'UI' half of the certificate and are missing from the surfaced content.

emergingTrends.rationale: Generative tools for ideation, copy, and layout (including AI features inside design tools) are reshaping UX workflows and should be introduced in an essentials certificate.
Scalable component libraries and design-to-code handoff are now standard practice that a modern UI/UX curriculum should teach.
Regulatory and ethical pressure is pushing accessibility from optional to foundational across UI/UX practice.
Immersive interfaces are a growth area the program could connect to XR design work already present elsewhere in this catalog (e.g., DD 650).
A/B testing, product analytics, and behavioral metrics increasingly guide UX decisions and would modernize the certificate.

topSuggestions: Fix the catalog data: the certificate currently lists 174 unrelated bioinformatics/CS courses and only 3 total credits; replace with the actual UI/UX course list (e.g., DD 640, IS 661, DD 634) and correct the credit total to ~12.
Resolve the 15 dangling course references and remove malformed prerequisite fields (e.g., CS 602's description-as-prerequisite) once the correct courses are loaded.
Publish 3-5 UI/UX learning outcomes covering user research, prototyping, usability testing, and accessible visual design.
Add explicit accessibility (WCAG) and design-systems/Figma prototyping content, baseline expectations the current entry does not show.
Add a plan-of-study block with required/elective structure so the credential is legible to prospective students and employers.
